title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Language Clinic: Score Ledger
summary: Score drifts from destruction records, waves advance before fragments resolve, the ship respawns inside danger, pacing accelerates without respite, and assistance modes shame the player. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the score ledger increment in fair progression and difficulty model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Specify response event ordering and test simultaneous projectile-rock, ship-rock, wave-clear, and life-loss cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-score-ledger — Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-score-progression-spawning-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-score-progression-spawning-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_score_progression_spawning_3(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Score Ledger evidence · Theory Score Ledger
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-score-ledger-vrcfrr" that uses case 8308 to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Score Ledger evidence · Theory Score Ledger.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Score Ledger into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Score Ledger evidence · Theory Score Ledger, keep Score Ledger connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-score-ledger-vrcfrr
# Competency: asteroids-score-ledger.
# Case 8308: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_score_ledger_vrcfrr(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vrcfrr = "vrcfrr-rrfcrv-8308"
    assert evidence_variant_vrcfrr
    evidence_axes_vrcfrr = (
        "playervrcfrr",
        "seed8308",
        "inputrrfcrv",
        "framevrcfrr8308",
        "state8308vrcfrr",
        "playtestrrfcrv8308",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vrcfrr)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Score Ledger model · Theory Score Ledger
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Score Ledger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9180 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Score Ledger model · Theory Score Ledger.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
